ORDER 1) TAKING PLAINTIFF MOHAMMED RAHMAN'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OFF CALENDAR AS MOOT; AND 2) GRANTING DEFENDANT MOTT'S LLP'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT

LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF MOHAMMED RAHMAN'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR, IN THE ALTERNATIVE, FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION OF ISSUES - DEFENDANT MOTT'S LLP'S SUMMARY JUDGMENT MOTION IS GRANTED AND PLAINTIFF MOHAMMED RAHMAN'S SUMMARY JUDGMENT MOTION IS THUS ORDERED OFF CALENDAR AS MOOT. PLAINTIFF CANNOT ESTABLISH THAT MOTT'S ENGAGED IN UNLAWFUL CONDUCT OR WAS UNJUSTLY ENRICHED, BECAUSE MOTT'S CONDUCT WAS LEGAL AS A MATTER OF LAW. ONE ORDER SIGNED FOR BOTH MOTIONS. [SEE WORD MINUTES AND ORDER FOR THE COURT'S COMPLETE RULING.] JUDGE: RICHARD B. ULMER, JR.; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; COURT REPORTER: MARIA A. TORREANO CSR#8600, EMAIL: MARIA.TORREANO@GMAIL.COM

THE COURT ORDERED THE FOLLOWING JUDGMENT ENTERED: IT IS ADJUDGED THAT PLAINTIFF RAHMAN, MOHAMMED , INDIVIDUALLY, AND ON BEHALF OF OTHER MEMBERS OF THE GENERAL PUBLIC SIMILARLY SITUATED TAKE NOTHING FROM DEFENDANT MOTT'S LLP, A DELAWARE LIMITED LIABILITY PARTNERSHIP SEE SCANNED DOCUMENT

MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar Feb-06-2020 Vacated

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, In The Alternative, For Summary Adjudication Of Issues
Off Calendar Jan-10-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances:
Steven Weinmann, Esq., of CAPSTONE LAW APC, T:310.712.8017, for plaintiff/moving party;
Jonathan Patchen, Esq. with Ariel House, Esq., of BAKER BOTTS, LLP, T:415.291.6209, for defendant/opposing party.

RULING: Plaintiff's counsel contested and argued the tentative ruling. After arguments, the Court adopted its tentative ruling on PLAINTIFF MOHAMMED RAHMAN's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, In The Alternative, For Summary Adjudication Of Issues as follows:

Defendant Mott's LLP's summary judgment motion is granted and plaintiff Mohammed Rahman's summary judgment motion is thus ordered off calendar as moot. Plaintiff cannot establish that Mott's engaged in unlawful conduct or was unjustly enriched, because Mott's conduct was legal as a matter of law.

[See word minutes for the Court's complete ruling.]

One order signed for both motions.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer, Jr.; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Maria A. Torreano CSR#8600, email: maria.torreano@gmail.com

Defendant Mott's LLP's summary judgment motion is granted and plaintiff Mohammed Rahman's summary judgment motion is thus ordered off calendar as moot. Plaintiff cannot establish that Mott's engaged in unlawful conduct or was unjustly enriched, because Mott's conduct was legal as a matter of law.
